text.tspan: Sept 2024: OpenAI o1. The model THINKS before responding.
Unlike CoT prompting, the reasoning happens at inference time
as a model behavior, not a prompt technique. Output: hidden
or visible 'thinking' tokens before the final answer.

g.text: When to use vs not use

text.tspan: OpenAI: o1 (Sept 2024) -> o1-pro -> o3 (Dec 2024) -> o3-pro -> o4-mini
Anthropic: Claude extended thinking (Feb 2025) on Sonnet/Opus 3.7+
Google: Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking, 2.5 Pro/Ultra Thinking
DeepSeek: R1 (Jan 2025) - open-weights reasoning
Alibaba: QwQ-32B (Nov 2024), Qwen-3-Thinking (2025)
xAI: Grok 4 Thinking
Meta, Mistral, AI21: various reasoning-tuned variants
Trained (via RL on reasoning traces) to produce a long
internal monologue BEFORE the final answer.
The monologue: tries approaches, catches errors, backtracks,
self-verifies, decomposes problems.
Usage cost: 5-100x more tokens at inference (thinking tokens).
Quality: large gains on math, code, planning, scientific reasoning.
Latency: 10s-minutes per response.
USE for: hard math, complex coding tasks, scientific analysis,
multi-step planning, RL agents, ambiguous decisions.
DON'T use for: simple lookups, chat, formatting, summarization,
anything where speed and cost matter more than quality.
Mixed strategy: route simple to fast models, hard to reasoning.
See brain-vs-hands.md.
